---
title: "Practical Program Evaluation"
description: "Book overview and study-note index for Practical Program Evaluation."
book_id: "practical-program-evaluation"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Book"
citation_key: "chen_2015_practical_program_evaluation"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:practical-program-evaluation:ch01-evaluation-design-and-its-components:c0002"
  - "chunk:practical-program-evaluation:ch03-logic-models-and-the-action-model-change-model-schema-program-theory:c0001"
  - "chunk:practical-program-evaluation:ch13-a-guide-to-productively-applying-the-logic-model-and-the-action-model-change-mod:c0001"
source_status: "needs_review"
---
<div class="landing-stack">

::: {.landing-hero}
<span class="hero-eyebrow">Book notes</span>

# Practical Program Evaluation

Study notes on *Practical Program Evaluation* [@chen_2015_practical_program_evaluation]. These notes are generated from source-linked MCP research-library records and are intended for study, comparison, and later refinement.

<div class="page-metadata">
  <span>3 major-section notes</span>
  <span>2015</span>
  <span>Source status: needs_review</span>
  <span>MCP evaluation-texts</span>
</div>
:::

::: {.section-grid}
::: {.section-card}
<span class="page-badge badge-synthesis">Synthesis</span>

### [Core Concepts](concepts.qmd)

Recurring ideas and distinctions used across these notes.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<span class="page-badge badge-map">Map</span>

### [Chapter Map](chapter-map.qmd)

Selected source sections, page ranges, and reading routes.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<span class="page-badge badge-practice">Practice</span>

### [Practice Implications](practice-implications.qmd)

Practical evaluation design habits drawn from the notes.
:::
:::

</div>

## Source

<div class="chapter-meta"><span>2015</span><span>MCP evaluation-texts</span><span>Status: needs_review</span><span>Book overview</span></div>

This index summarises generated study notes for Huey T. Chen's *Practical Program Evaluation* [@chen_2015_practical_program_evaluation]. The generated pages use selected MCP research-library retrieval records and compact evidence summaries.

## Core Argument

Evaluation design should match a program's stage, stakeholder needs, and theory of how action is expected to produce change.

## Major-Section Notes

- [Evaluation Design and Its Components](section-01-evaluation-design-components.qmd) - source pages 39-40
- [Logic Models and the Action Model/Change Model Schema](section-02-logic-models-and-program-theory.qmd) - source pages 79
- [When Logic Models Do Not Work as Expected](section-03-logic-model-limits.qmd) - source pages 382

## References
